assets/hero/chummer6-hero.png.visual_prompt: First-contact Shadowrun cover scene in a cramped streetdoc intake bay or runner prep cage, never an alley and never a tabletop. One standing runner works a vertical prep wall crowded with hanging gear rails, clipped med tags, sealed pouches, harness straps, intake bins, translucent stat markers, med drawers, and proof anchors while build-state provenance traces and fit-check brackets cling to those physical props. The frame must feel dense, layered, and immediately specific to character-build trust, rules inspection pressure, and Chummer-style visible reasoning. Push it toward packed flashy cover energy: stronger orange-cyan contrast, harder rim light, bolder foreground clutter, and obvious foreground-midground-background separation. Show the runner in three-quarter or over-shoulder posture with both arms actively sorting gear and tags, surrounded by foreground clutter on both sides. Strong foreground props, readable midground posture, rich background clues, no readable text, no table scene, no desk glamour, no cards or paper in hand, no phone hero prop, no empty darkness, and no quiet side-profile portrait.

scene_contract.subject: one standing runner deciding whether tonight's build can be trusted before stepping back into the rain
scene_contract.environment: a cramped streetdoc intake bay or runner prep cage packed with hanging gear rails, clipped med tags, sealed med pouches, harness straps, intake bins, med drawers, translucent rule markers, and hard practical spill
scene_contract.action: pausing mid-prep with both hands on hanging gear and clipped tags while layered trust traces, fit checks, and proof anchors live through the room around them
scene_contract.mood: tense, specific, and high-pressure instead of brooding

scene_contract.environment: a scarred desk with hazard tape, scattered notes, and one unforgiving task lamp
scene_contract.action: daring the viewer to stop reading and risk their evening

scene_contract.easter_egg_visibility: visible on a README banner without stealing the shot
